notes.energy: Strong defender of Wyoming's coal, oil, and natural gas industries
notes.education: Weighing federal school voucher program participation (2025)
notes.fiscal: Conservative fiscal management of state budget
notes.land_management: Opposes federal overreach on Wyoming lands

profile.religion: Not publicly specified (family has Unitarian background)
profile.background: Rancher raised on family ranch in Johnson County, Wyoming; Wyoming State Treasurer (2012-2019); became 33rd governor in 2019.

notes.senate_leadership: Senate Republican Conference Chair (No. 3 leadership position)
notes.energy: Chairs Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee, defends fossil fuels
notes.religious_liberty: Opposed Obama-era Obamacare mandate targeting religious freedom

profile.education: Georgetown University (BS Biology), Georgetown University School of Medicine (MD), Yale Medical School (residency)
profile.background: Board-certified orthopedic surgeon who practiced in Casper, Wyoming for 24 years. Former Wyoming State Senator. Senate Majority Whip since 2025. Appointed to Senate in 2007.
profile.office: 307 Dirksen Senate Office Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Wyoming delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

notes.bitcoin: Leading Bitcoin/crypto advocate in Senate. Introduced BITCOIN Act of 2025 establishing Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. Personally owns Bitcoin (disclosed purchase up to $100K in 2021)
notes.retirement_note: Will NOT seek re-election in 2026, endorsed Harriet Hageman as successor

profile.education: University of Wyoming (BS Animal Science 1976, BS Biology 1978, JD 1985)
profile.background: Attorney, rancher, and former Wyoming State Treasurer. Former U.S. House member and Wyoming state legislator. First woman to represent Wyoming in the Senate. Bitcoin advocate.
profile.office: 127a Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 100 East B Street, Suite 3201, Dick Cheney Federal Building

notes.liz_cheney: Defeated Liz Cheney in 2022 Republican primary with Trump endorsement
notes.constitutional: Strong constitutional conservative, member of Federalist Society
notes.water_rights: Natural resources attorney specializing in water and land use law
notes.federal_overreach: Opposes federal land management overreach in Wyoming
notes.senate_run_2026: Running for Lummis Senate seat in 2026, endorsed by Barrasso and Lummis

profile.education: University of Wyoming (BA Business Administration 1986, JD 1989)
